title: Chapter Seventy-Eight: The Seat of Honour
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Above the practice ground, in the seat of honour, two old powers watched.

"The Sect Lord?" the Elder Council Lord said, stroking the white beard that hung below his chin. His purple robes were magnificent. His presence was no less than Ma Yeon-jeok's beside him.

"Haven't seen her face in a long time."

"It's only been seven days and nights. Mind your own family affairs. I heard there was quite a disturbance."

Ma Yeon-jeok's face darkened. "I just came back furious. Before being an imperial family, she's my family member, yet acting so rashly--"

"Isn't she your nephew's wife? Can't just call it rash."

"I'm greatly disappointed."

The Elder Council Lord pointed down at the practice ground where Lightning Genius Jeong Yeon-shin had just won the semifinal match. The youth in blue martial robes was slowly sheathing his sword. No trembling in the motion. Calm expression. Sixteen years old.

"Look at their faces," the Elder Council Lord said. "White Qilin, raised in comfort -- smiles seeping from his lips. Pleasant to see. Very natural. But Lightning Genius? His expression is so absent it seems he grew up being hit. Those chilling eyes." He smiled. "A face good for killing people."

"Enough," Ma Yeon-jeok said.

"No one can beat someone who smiles. White Qilin has everything -- perfect childhood, qualities, spirit medicines filling his meridians since birth. He might have become Namgung family head if not for his brother."

"He might already be comparable to White Qilin." Ma Yeon-jeok's voice was quiet. Certain. "Yeon-shin was born with an unprecedented sense. Martial arts created with great master's qualities fit him perfectly. I can't even guess how much more he'll grow."

"Then why don't you take him in? You seem unable to even lure him with wealth."

Silence.

"I can roughly guess," the Elder Council Lord said, broad smile widening. "You're afraid. Of going against the mindset of the grandson you abandoned. Or perhaps the Sect Lord scolded you?"

No answer.

"Both! How pitiful karma can be."

Ma Yeon-jeok looked down at his grandson. The final match was imminent. Lightning Genius and White Qilin.

"Yeon-shin will become the main fortress's divine sword," he murmured. "Ma family martial arts don't suit him. I must think of another way."

Below, the two young masters faced each other.

---

Yeon-shin spread his palm.

The combination of Demon Roar Blood Energy and Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm blazed from his left hand -- cold as ice, fierce as a heartbeat. Blue light pulsed, wrapping his fingers, his knuckles, his wrist.

BANG.

The shockwave drove Hwa-sin back. Dust erupted. The training ground shook.

*The power is certain. But precision dropped. An issue to resolve before we reach the Namgung family.*

Right now, though, confusion was enough.

In the instant Hwa-sin regained his balance, Yeon-shin planted his foot and launched. One step closed five paces. The Desolate Sword came up with Radiant Sword Style's horizontal strike, twisting through the distorting light.

CLANG.

Azure Sky Boundless Sword met him. Their blades locked. Hwa-sin's force grew, pushing, crushing -- the raw weight of a scion raised on noble family medicines.

Yeon-shin yielded.

Deliberately. He spun in place, loaded Battle Vision into his fist, and drove Spiral into Hwa-sin's flank. A thud. But Hwa-sin's body technique was sublime -- Unhindered Clear Flow Body carried him through and past, his robe swirling in the energy wake before spreading out again.

*Shallow.*

And there -- the sting of Hwa-sin's counter across his thigh.

They closed again. Azure Sky Boundless Sword spread wide from an upper stance, majestic and magnificent. Multiple sword energies imbued in a single strike, approaching like a net covering the sky.

*Dodge and cut through.*

Radiant Wing Step. First Step -- the direction of Hwa-sin's sword path blazed clear in his mind. One footstep that could dance between the sword energy net. He stepped exactly so.

STEP.

Sword force aftermath cut his shoulder cloth. He advanced through it.

STEP.

Second Step. Colourless waves pushed back the scattering energies. The distance closed.

He raised the Desolate Sword. Hwa-sin met him. Wind streamed along both blades.

CLANG.

Heavy recoil numbed his grip. He suppressed a groan. The gap was narrow -- this meant only blows.

CLANG. CLANG. THUD.

Radiant Sword Style. Radiant Wing Step. Eternal Blossom Fist -- Spiral, Advancing Thunder, Blossom Fist. He unleashed everything, every martial art he had created and mastered. He made this place a forge. Even if blood sprayed. Even if wounds opened.

When Hwa-sin brought fearsome counters born from hours of observation, Yeon-shin changed verses on the spot. When Hwa-sin adapted, Yeon-shin adapted faster.

The crowd went silent. The training ground became a cathedral of violence and beauty, two geniuses burning at the apex of their craft.

*I feel it. The martial arts -- seeping through my entire body. Growing sharper with every exchange.*

His techniques rose endlessly more precise. The wind wrapping his extended hand was gentle now. Hwa-sin's sword energy rippled with Eternal Blossom Fist's Advancing Thunder and split at the end of Radiant Wing Step.

Something awakened. An old feeling, familiar and fierce.

The boy smiled.

---
